claim: Evidence schema, prompt, readiness contract, and tests all distinguish project, external, user, and unknown evidence scopes.

evidence.summary: DEMAND_EVIDENCE_RESULT_SCHEMA_DEFINITION defines four valid scopes with documentation
evidence.why: Schema contract explicitly states scope is project | external | user | unknown and that project facts require project-scoped evidence with repo-relative paths.

evidence.summary: VALID_EVIDENCE_SCOPES constant declared with four scope values
evidence.why: Independent confirmation of the Set containing project, external, user, unknown.

evidence.summary: evidenceRecordScope classifies records into four scopes with alias mapping
evidence.why: Runtime logic maps aliases (repo/repository/codebase/local to project, web/internet/research to external, human/operator to user) and returns unknown for unverified.

evidence.summary: evidenceRecordHasProjectLocator validates project evidence has non-URL path
evidence.why: Project evidence requires a repo-relative path; URLs are rejected.

evidence.summary: evidenceScopeDeclarationIssues blocks evidence without valid scope
evidence.why: Readiness gate prevents evidence without explicit scope from satisfying PRD requirements.

evidence.summary: projectEvidenceBlockingIssues enforces project evidence path requirements
evidence.why: Blocks external-only or unknown evidence from satisfying existing-project facts; requires project scope with concrete path.

evidence.summary: inspectDemandPrdReadiness validates scope compliance for each agent result
evidence.why: Readiness contract checks evidenceScopeDeclarationIssues and projectEvidenceBlockingIssues for each complete evidence result.
Dispatch-level validation uses the same four-scope constant.

evidence.summary: evidenceScopeErrors validates each evidence record declares valid scope
evidence.why: Agent result validation fails if scope is not one of the four valid values.

evidence.summary: Agent prompt hard rules enumerate scope and path requirements
evidence.why: Prompt tells agents that evidence records must include scope and project facts require repo-relative paths.

evidence.summary: Required JSON shape documents four scope values in agent output schema
evidence.why: Agent output contract defines scope field as project | external | user | unknown.

evidence.summary: Test verifies external-only evidence cannot satisfy existing project facts
evidence.why: Contract test confirms that when all agents return scope=external evidence, PRD readiness fails with PROJECT_FACT_REQUIRES_PROJECT_EVIDENCE blocker.

evidence.summary: Test verifies project evidence requires concrete repo-relative path
evidence.why: Contract test confirms that project-scoped evidence without a path triggers PROJECT_EVIDENCE_PATH_REQUIRED blocker.

evidence.summary: Test verifies external URLs cannot be relabeled as project evidence
evidence.why: Contract test confirms that evidence with scope=project but a URL field triggers PROJECT_EVIDENCE_PATH_REQUIRED blocker.

evidence.summary: Test verifies evidence records must declare valid scope explicitly
evidence.why: Contract test confirms that evidence without a scope field triggers EVIDENCE_SCOPE_REQUIRED blocker.

risks: If evidence agents misclassify web research as project scope, the path requirement should still catch URLs and block them
If agents omit scope entirely, evidenceScopeDeclarationIssues correctly blocks readiness

result.notes: Independent cross-check confirms all four components explicitly distinguish and enforce the four evidence scopes. Schema definition (router.ts:37-50), agent prompt (evidence-dispatch.ts:343-388), readiness contract (router.ts:438-530, 709-733), and tests (demand-router.test.ts:407-591) all implement scope validation correctly. No conflicts found with explorer results.
